rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Release Of Property From Claim Of Mechanics Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 29, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER KARA MORRISON'S PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM CLAIM OF MECHANICS LIEN is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KALCO PROPERTIES, INC. VS. DAVID HUNTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 29, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is DENIED. Plaintiff cited no authority for the proposition that verification is not proper by a Power of Attorney. Drake v. Superior Court (1994)21 Cal.App.4th 1826 contains no such holding and appears to cite in dicta with approval Attorney General opinion distinguishing limited powers given by Probate Code 4459(e) (formerly Civil Code 2994(e)), including the power to verify pleadings, and plenary power to practice law. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KALCO PROPERTIES, INC. VS. DAVID HUNTER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 29, 2022 line 5. PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the 7th affirmative defense. Demurrer will not be sustained only to a part of the defense (subsidy) and demurrer raises factual dispute (waiver/reliance, etc). Demurrer is otherwise sustained with leave to amend (except for defenses that have been withdrawn). Defendant shall have 5 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended answer. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
